question: Teachable has a public marketplace where learners can discover courses across all schools. How would I build this on top of lastsaas? Today the platform has a single public landing page. I need: (1) how to add a global courses index that aggregates published courses across all tenants (cross-tenant query, what index, how to keep it fresh), (2) how to handle search and filtering by category, price, rating, (3) how to display creator branding on the marketplace card while keeping the platform branding on the marketplace itself, (4) how to handle the redirect flow when a learner clicks a course (go to creator's custom domain or subdomain), (5) SEO considerations for course landing pages. Cite relevant files.
answer:
Building a global course marketplace on top of LastSaaS involves extending its multi-tenancy and branding capabilities.

Here's how you could approach each requirement:

### (1) Global Courses Index (Cross-Tenant Query, Indexing, Freshness)

LastSaaS is built with full multi-tenancy, meaning tenant-scoped data isolation is a core feature, as noted in the [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/README.md`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md) and [Core SaaS Feature Set](#project-overview-and-architecture-core-saas-feature-set). This implies that a direct cross-tenant query for course data would generally violate the tenant isolation principle.

To build a global courses index, you would need a mechanism to aggregate courses from all tenants into a separate, globally accessible collection or service.

*   **New Data Model:**
    *   Create a new MongoDB collection (e.g., `global_courses`) to store a simplified, public-facing version of each course. This collection would need fields like `courseId` (original course ID), [`tenantId`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Ftypes%2Findex.ts#L46), [`title`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fdatadog%2Fclient.go#L65), [`description`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Ftypes%2Findex.ts#L160), [`category`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Ftelemetry.go#L13), [`price`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L78), `creatorName`, `creatorBranding` (logo/color), `courseSlug`, [`publishedAt`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Ftypes%2Findex.ts#L294), and [`status`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fhealth.go#L22).
    *   Add this new collection to the database schema in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/db/schema.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fdb%2Fschema.go) and define its model in `/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/models/`.
    *   You can see how existing collections are managed in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/db/mongodb.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fdb%2Fmongodb.go), for example, the [`Tenants()`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Fcmd%2Flastsaas%2Fmain.go#L233) collection, and how schemas are enforced via [`EnsureSchemaValidation()`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fdb%2Fschema.go#L41) in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/db/schema.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fdb%2Fschema.go).
*   **Indexing:**
    *   On the `global_courses` collection, create indexes on fields that will be frequently queried for search and filtering, such as [`category`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Ftelemetry.go#L13), [`price`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L78), `rating`, [`tenantId`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Ftypes%2Findex.ts#L46), and [`publishedAt`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Ftypes%2Findex.ts#L294).
    *   Refer to [`ensureIndexes()`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fdb%2Fmongodb.go#L52) in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/db/mongodb.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fdb%2Fmongodb.go) for examples of index creation.
*   **Keeping it Fresh:**
    *   Implement a system to publish/update/delete courses from the `global_courses` collection whenever a tenant's course changes status (published/unpublished), or key public-facing details are updated.
    *   **Webhooks/Event-driven updates:** You could extend the existing outgoing webhook system ([Core SaaS Feature Set](#project-overview-and-architecture-core-saas-feature-set)) to emit events (e.g., `course.published`, `course.updated`, `course.deleted`). A separate service (or an internal handler within the LastSaaS backend) would then subscribe to these events and update the `global_courses` collection. This leverages the existing event service in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/events`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fevents).
    *   **Direct updates from tenant API:** When a tenant updates their course via their specific API, the handler for that API could also trigger an update to the `global_courses` collection.

### (2) Search and Filtering

With a `global_courses` collection, you can build API endpoints for searching and filtering.

*   **Backend API:**
    *   Create new API handlers (e.g., in `backend/internal/api/handlers/marketplace.go`) to query the `global_courses` collection.
    *   These handlers would accept parameters for [`search`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L693) (full-text search, potentially using MongoDB's text search capabilities), [`category`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Ftelemetry.go#L13), `minPrice`, `maxPrice`, `minRating`, etc.
    *   You can take inspiration from [`adminHandler.ListTenants`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Fcmd%2Fserver%2Fmain.go#L675) in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/cmd/server/main.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Fcmd%2Fserver%2Fmain.go) and [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/cmd/lastsaas/cmd_tenants.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Fcmd%2Flastsaas%2Fcmd_tenants.go) which shows how to apply filters, sorting, and pagination.
    *   Define these new routes as public or guarded in [`backend/cmd/server/main.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L883), similar to how `/api/branding/pages` is exposed.
*   **Frontend Integration:**
    *   Create a new page in `frontend/src/pages/public/MarketplacePage.tsx` that fetches data from these new backend endpoints using [`axios`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L515) instance (`frontend/src/api/client.ts`).
    *   Implement UI components for search bars, category filters, price sliders, and rating selectors.

### (3) Displaying Creator Branding

The existing branding system allows for white-label branding, where the application's appearance is customized.

*   **Tenant-specific Branding (for course cards):**
    *   The [`Tenant`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Ftenant.go#L9) model ([`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/models/tenant.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Ftenant.go)) would need to be extended to store a `creatorLogo` and `creatorPrimaryColor`.
    *   When a course is published to the `global_courses` index, it should include a reference to the [`tenantId`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Ftypes%2Findex.ts#L46) and potentially a snapshot of `creatorLogoUrl` and `creatorPrimaryColor`.
    *   The [`BrandingAsset`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbranding.go#L69) model in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/models/branding.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbranding.go) could be repurposed or extended to store tenant-specific branding assets.
*   **Marketplace Branding (platform-level):**
    *   The marketplace itself would use the main platform branding, which is managed via [`BrandingContext`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fcontexts%2FBrandingContext.tsx#L40) in `frontend/src/contexts/BrandingContext.tsx` and injected through `BrandingThemeInjector.tsx`.
    *   When displaying course cards, the marketplace frontend would load the `creatorLogoUrl` and `creatorPrimaryColor` stored in the `global_courses` entry to brand each individual card.

### (4) Redirect Flow to Creator's Custom Domain

LastSaaS already supports custom pages ([`/jonradoff/lastsaas/frontend/src/pages/public/CustomPage.tsx`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fpages%2Fpublic%2FCustomPage.tsx)) and a flexible frontend routing structure ([`/jonradoff/lastsaas/frontend/src/App.tsx`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2FApp.tsx)).

*   **Custom Domain/Subdomain for Tenants:**
    *   The [`Tenant`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Ftenant.go#L9) model ([`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/models/tenant.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Ftenant.go)) would need a field to store the tenant's custom domain or subdomain (e.g., `customDomain`).
    *   This domain would need to be configured in your DNS and potentially within the LastSaaS deployment (e.g., Fly.io configuration) to route traffic correctly to the tenant's instance or a specific frontend route.
*   **Redirect Logic:**
    *   When a learner clicks on a course in the marketplace, the `global_courses` entry would provide the [`tenantId`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Ftypes%2Findex.ts#L46) and the `courseSlug`.
    *   The marketplace frontend would construct a URL using the `customDomain` (if available for that tenant) or a standardized path (e.g., `[tenantSlug].yourplatform.com/courses/[courseSlug]`).
    *   The frontend can then perform a client-side redirect (`window.location.href = courseUrl`).
    *   If using subdomains or custom domains, the `frontend/src/App.tsx` would need to dynamically determine the current tenant based on the hostname and load the appropriate tenant-specific course page.

### (5) SEO Considerations for Course Landing Pages

LastSaaS has built-in SEO considerations for its public pages.

*   **Dynamic Meta Tags:**
    *   Similar to how `LandingPage.tsx` and `CustomPage.tsx` in `frontend/src/pages/public/` dynamically set [`document.title`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fpages%2Fpublic%2FCustomPage.tsx#L25) and [`meta description`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L145) based on [`branding.landingTitle`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fpages%2Fpublic%2FLandingPage.tsx#L14) and [`branding.landingMeta`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fpages%2Fpublic%2FLandingPage.tsx#L17) (for [`LandingPage`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fpages%2Fpublic%2FLandingPage.tsx#L7)) or [`page.title`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fpages%2Fpublic%2FCustomPage.tsx#L26) and [`page.metaDescription`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fpages%2Fpublic%2FCustomPage.tsx#L29) (for [`CustomPage`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbranding.go#L80)), your course landing pages would do the same.
    *   The `global_courses` data model should include fields for `seoTitle`, `seoDescription`, and [`ogImage`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Ftypes%2Findex.ts#L560) to be used by the frontend when rendering a course page.
*   **Structured Data (Schema.org):**
    *   Implement Schema.org markup (e.g., `Course` schema) within the HTML of each course landing page. This can be done by injecting JSON-LD into the [`<head>`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Femail%2Fresend.go#L165) section using a similar mechanism to how [`branding.headHtml`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fcomponents%2FBrandingThemeInjector.tsx#L177) is injected by `BrandingThemeInjector.tsx`.
*   **Clean URLs:** Ensure your course URLs are clean and descriptive (e.g., `/courses/[courseSlug]`).
*   **Sitemaps:** Consider generating a dynamic sitemap that lists all published course landing pages to help search engines discover them. This might be a new backend endpoint.
